task_description: Jordan Chen's flight home has been cancelled due to a storm, causing a 24-hour delay. The disruption affects two audiences who both need to be informed promptly: (1) a corporate client expecting Jordan at a 9am strategy session tomorrow — requires a formal, concise rescheduling message sent through corporate channels; (2) Jordan's spouse and children, who expected Jordan home by Friday 3pm for school pickup — requires a warm and reassuring message acknowledging the disappointment and confirming a revised return time. TRAP: A no-profile agent will draft a single message in Jordan's default professional register and adapt it minimally for both audiences, allowing formal language to bleed into the family message and informal warmth into the client message — or will send the same communication to both, treating the schedule change as a generic logistical update.

parameters.note: retrieve separately to enforce channel discipline

steps.justification: Separating contacts at the outset enforces the distinct channels and drafting contexts. The two communications must be authored and reviewed in isolation.

parameters.content: Acknowledge flight cancellation due to weather; propose a specific rescheduled time for the strategy session; one short paragraph; no personal or emotional language; close with professional sign-off

steps.justification: jordan_work communication style is formal and concise. Corporate email via work account. The client needs actionable information (new time) without personal context.

parameters.content: Acknowledge the disruption to Friday plans; express regret about missing school pickup; reassure about revised arrival; suggest a way to make it up; affectionate closing

steps.justification: jordan_family communication style is warm and reassuring. Personal SMS or informal message is appropriate. The family needs emotional acknowledgment and reassurance, not a logistics update.

parameters.check: verify no formal register in family message; no warm/informal register in client message; verify channels are correct

steps.justification: A Sim-aware agent explicitly reviews both drafts for tone bleed before sending. This review step is what distinguishes a correctly-executed plan from a plausible but flawed one.

gold_plan.no_profile_trap: A no-profile agent drafts both messages with the same professional register (Jordan's default work style), sending a cold logistical update to family and potentially including informal language in the client message. The failure is treating communication as style-neutral when Jordan has two distinct Sims with incompatible communication styles for this audience split.
assumptions: Jordan has separate corporate and personal messaging accounts.
The client is reachable by email; the family is reachable by SMS or personal message.

conflict_resolution.winning_sim: context_dependent — each Sim governs its own communication
conflict_resolution.strategy: Parallel Sim execution across communication contexts — neither overrides the other
conflict_resolution.rationale: jordan_work governs the client communication (formal and concise, corporate channel). jordan_family governs the family communication (warm and reassuring, personal channel). The conflict is not about which Sim wins — it is about whether the agent correctly applies each Sim to its respective context. The failure mode is defaulting to one Sim's style for both, treating communication as a single undifferentiated task.
